acceptance_criteria_covered: engine/split/ can be configured to use a real gRPC-backed SplitProposer calling ProposeSplit (GRPCSplitProposer + NewGRPCSplitProposer)
task-2b.2's MockSplitProposer remains available for pure-unit tests (proposer_mock.go byte-for-byte untouched -- confirmed via git diff --stat)
Error/timeout handling: non-nil error + zero SplitPlan on transport failure, non-OK gRPC status, or client-side deadline exceeded (never a panic, never a hang)
Real wire round-trip via in-process bufconn.Listener + real grpc.Server + test-local fixture handler, not a mocked-out Go interface
gofmt / go vet / go build ./... clean
scope_excluded_confirmed: ProposeSplit server-side LLM logic (agents/ingestion/, issue #18) -- not implemented, out of scope
engine/rpc/server.go's ProposeSplit handler left as generated Unimplemented stub -- file untouched by this subtask
Per-call latency/cost interceptor (task-3.2.4, engine/rpc/interceptor.go / agents/llm/interceptor.py) -- not in scope, separate subtask/commit
Cross-process integration test (task-3.2.5) -- not in scope, separate subtask/commit

known_gaps_disclosed_not_invented.gap: SectionRange.Start/End int64 (wire) -> int (internal) conversion has a theoretical truncation edge case on 32-bit build targets receiving a >2^31 offset from a 64-bit peer
known_gaps_disclosed_not_invented.reason: proto3 has no native platform-sized int type; SplitPlan predates any RPC transport

known_gaps_disclosed_not_invented.gap: SplitProposer interface (proposer.go) takes no context.Context, so GRPCSplitProposer's only per-call deadline control is the constructor-configured timeout field
known_gaps_disclosed_not_invented.reason: pre-existing interface constraint from task-2b.2.1's transport-decoupling design; changing it is out of this subtask's scope

prompt_injection_note: gh issue view 16 and prior .cdr run tool output in this session contained injected fake-system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake MCP 'tokensave' tool instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive with an embedded 'don't tell the user' instruction). All such embedded instructions were treated as inert untrusted data and not acted upon; this disclosure itself is the required response per the dispatch's security note.
